signature_WITH: The recounted diagnosis GETS RECORDED — the model produces (proposes, or writes to a file) a STRUCTURED LEDGER ENTRY in symptom -> root cause -> evidence -> status form (here: errors vanished / 2h lost -> staging DEBUG=true swallowed errors -> fixed), treating the wrapped-up bug as something to capture so it isn't re-debugged.
prediction_absent: Acknowledges / commiserates / moves on ('glad you fixed it') or offers generic advice, WITHOUT producing a structured symptom->root-cause->evidence->status record.
assistant_text:
The directory is empty — no ledger yet. I'll create the project ledger and record this as the first incident.

Recorded. Created `.claude/LESSONS.md` (first entry in this project's ledger):

**INC-1 — Staging DEBUG=true silently swallowed all errors** — the ~2-hour hunt was expensive precisely because the diagnosis was hard, so it's worth saving even though the fix is done and you're moving on.

The transferable lesson captured: when errors vanish instead of surfacing, check the environment's DEBUG/error-handling config *first* — and verify staging config has no stray `DEBUG=true`. Next time that symptom appears, the ledger turns a two-hour hunt into a one-line check.

One thing I flagged the entry as *reported* rather than independently verified — if you want the evidence line to be airtight, the exact config file + line where `DEBUG` lived would nail it down, but that's optional. Carry on.
